{"key_event":"Founding of Indian National Congress","reasoning":"The British Raj had a strong colonial bureaucracy, army, courts, railways, and revenue apparatus, though its reach still depended partly on princes, landlords, and local intermediaries. Civil society was emerging through the press, reform associations, and the early Indian National Congress, but mobilization was largely elite, urban, and petitionary rather than mass-based.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":4.0,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":7.5}
